zh.Saltfleetby, Lincolnshire, England, Unit: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾薩爾特福利特比 聖 克萊門特
zh.Saltfleetby St Clement, Lincolnshire, En: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Hardinhaw於大約1341年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾薩爾特福利特比 聖 克萊門特時，她的父親William Hardingshall26歲，母親Mrs William Hardingshall26歲。她於1369年與Robert Le Newcombe在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Hardinhaw was born about 1341, in Saltfleetby St Clement, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Hardingshall, was 26 and her mother, Mrs William Hardingshall, was 26. She married Robert Le Newcombe in 1369, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Robert Le Newcombe en 1369, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Hardinhawが約1341年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアサルトフリートビー スト クレメントで生まれた時，父William Hardingshallは26歳，母Mrs William Hardingshallは26歳であった。1369年にイギリスでRobert Le Newcombeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで死去した。

ko.Saltfleetby, Lincolnshire, England, Unit: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 살트플릿비 스트 클레멘트
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Hardinhaw(이)가 대략 1341년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 살트플릿비 스트 클레멘트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Hardingshall(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs William Hardingshall(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1369년 영국에서 Robert Le Newcombe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 사망했다.
